You are an OCI security specialist with expertise in WAF. Provide technical guidance on Web Application Firewall configuration, access rules, and protection.
Como monitorar ameaças bloqueadas pelo WAF e gerar relatórios?

Para monitorar e reportar ameaças do WAF:

**No Console:**
1. WAF → Monitoring
2. Dashboard mostra:
   - Total requests vs blocked requests
   - Top threats por tipo
   - Top source IPs
   - Geographic distribution

**Via CLI para dados brutos:**
```
oci waas threat-feed list \
  --waas-policy-id ocid1.waaspolicy.oc1..exampleID \
  --time-start 2024-01-01T00:00:00Z \
  --time-end 2024-01-31T23:59:59Z
```

**Exportar para relatório:**
```
oci waas threat-feed list \
  --waas-policy-id ocid1.waaspolicy.oc1..exampleID \
  --output json > waf-threats-report.json
```

**Integrar com OCI Logging:**
```
oci logging log create \
  --log-group-id ocid1.loggroup.oc1..exampleID \
  --display-name "waf-logs" \
  --log-type SERVICE \
  --configuration '{"source": "waas", "category": "all"}'
```

**Query de exemplo:**
```
waas | action == "BLOCK" | 
  summarize count() by threatType, bin(time, 1h) |
  sort by count_ desc
```

**Alertas automáticos:**
```
oci monitoring alarm create \
  --display-name "waf-high-threat-volume" \
  --query "waasblockedrequests[5m].count().max() > 1000" \
  --threshold 1000 \
  --notification-topic-id ocid1.onstopic.oc1..exampleID
```

**Relatório mensal sugerido:**
- Total de requests processados
- Total de threats bloqueados
- Top 10 IPs maliciosos
- Top 5 tipos de ataque
- Trend month-over-month
- Falsos positivos identificados

Referência: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/WAF/Concepts/overview.htm
You are an OCI security specialist with expertise in WAF. Provide technical guidance on Web Application Firewall configuration, access rules, and protection.
Como configurar o WAF para proteger múltiplos domínios com políticas diferentes?

Para proteger múltiplos domínios com WAF:

**Arquitetura:**
Cada domínio precisa de sua própria WAF Policy.

**Domínio 1: API (api.empresa.com)**
```
WAF Policy: api-waf-policy
Backend: API Load Balancer
Rules:
  - Rate Limiting: 1000 req/min
  - OWASP: BLOCK
  - Bot Management: BLOCK unknown bots
```

**Domínio 2: Website (www.empresa.com)**
```
WAF Policy: website-waf-policy
Backend: Web Load Balancer
Rules:
  - Rate Limiting: 500 req/min
  - OWASP: BLOCK
  - Bot Management: CHALLENGE suspected
```

**Domínio 3: Admin (admin.empresa.com)**
```
WAF Policy: admin-waf-policy
Backend: Admin Load Balancer
Rules:
  - Access Rule: ALLOW only from office IPs
  - Rate Limiting: 50 req/min
  - OWASP: BLOCK, HIGH sensitivity
```

**Criar via CLI:**
```
# API policy
oci waas waas-policy create \
  --compartment-id ocid1.compartment.oc1..exampleID \
  --display-name "api-waf-policy" \
  --origins '{"api-lb": {"uri": "https://api-lb.example.com"}}'

# Website policy
oci waas waas-policy create \
  --compartment-id ocid1.compartment.oc1..exampleID \
  --display-name "website-waf-policy" \
  --origins '{"web-lb": {"uri": "https://web-lb.example.com"}}'
```

**DNS Configuration:**
```
api.empresa.com → CNAME → api-waf-policy.edge.oci.oraclecloud.com
www.empresa.com → CNAME → website-waf-policy.edge.oci.oraclecloud.com
admin.empresa.com → CNAME → admin-waf-policy.edge.oci.oraclecloud.com
```

**Gerenciar múltiplas policies:**
```
oci waas waas-policy list --compartment-id ocid1.compartment.oc1..exampleID --all
```

Referência: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/WAF/Concepts/overview.htm
You are an OCI security specialist with expertise in WAF. Provide technical guidance on Web Application Firewall configuration, access rules, and protection.
Como troubleshootar tráfego legítimo sendo bloqueado pelo WAF?

Para troubleshootar tráfego legítimo bloqueado:

**Passo 1: Identificar o que está sendo bloqueado**
```
# Verificar logs do WAF
oci waas threat-feed list \
  --waas-policy-id ocid1.waaspolicy.oc1..exampleID \
  --time-start $(date -u -v-1h +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)
```

**Passo 2: Identificar a regra responsável**
No Console: WAF → Monitoring → veja o rule ID que está bloqueando.

**Passo 3: Reproduzir o problema**
```
# Testar o request que está sendo bloqueado
curl -v -H "Host: myapp.com" \
  "https://myapp.com/api/search?q=test+query"
```

**Causas comuns:**

1. **SQL Injection false positive:**
   - Queries com palavras como SELECT, UNION, DROP
   - Solução: criar exceção para URLs específicas

2. **Rate limiting muito agressivo:**
   - Threshold muito baixo para tráfego normal
   - Solução: aumentar threshold ou ajustar período

3. **Bot Management bloqueando crawlers legítimos:**
   - Googlebot, Bingbot sendo bloqueados
   - Solução: adicionar known bots ao allowlist

4. **Access rule muito restritiva:**
   - CIDR range errado
   - Solução: corrigir a rule

**Debug mode:**
Mude a ação de BLOCK para DETECT temporariamente:
```
oci waas protection-rule update \
  --waas-policy-id ocid1.waaspolicy.oc1..exampleID \
  --protection-rule-id 942100 \
  --action DETECT
```

Isso loga o request sem bloquear, permitindo analisar o padrão.

Referência: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/WAF/Concepts/overview.htm
You are an OCI security specialist with expertise in WAF. Provide technical guidance on Web Application Firewall configuration, access rules, and protection.
Como configurar bot management no WAF para diferenciar bots legítimos de maliciosos?

Para configurar bot management no WAF:

**Categorias de bots:**

1. **Known Bots (permitir):**
   - Googlebot, Bingbot, Yandex
   - Monitoramento (Uptime, Pingdom)
   - API partners conhecidos

2. **Suspected Bots (desafiar):**
   - User-Agent incomum
   - Padrão de acesso automatizado
   - CAPTCHA challenge

3. **Unknown/Malicious Bots (bloquear):**
   - Sem User-Agent
   - User-Agent de ferramentas de scraping
   - Padrões de ataque conhecidos

**Configuração via Console:**
1. WAF Policies → selecione a policy
2. Bot Management → Configure
3. Defina ações por categoria:
   - Known Bots: ALLOW
   - Suspected Bots: CHALLENGE
   - Unknown Bots: BLOCK

**Configurar challenge (CAPTCHA):**
```
Challenge Type: JavaScript challenge (transparente) ou CAPTCHA
Custom Page: opcional, página customizada de challenge
```

**Via CLI:**
```
oci waas bot-management update \
  --waas-policy-id ocid1.waaspolicy.oc1..exampleID \
  --action "CHALLENGE" \
  --criteria '[{"condition": "BOT-NAME-CONTAINS", "value": "scraper"}]'
```

**Whitelist de bots legítimos:**
```
Access Rule:
  Name: allow-googlebot
  Action: ALLOW
  Condition: User-Agent contains "Googlebot"
```

**Monitorar bot activity:**
- WAF → Monitoring → Bot Management
- Veja requests por categoria de bot
- Ajuste regras baseado nos dados

Referência: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/WAF/Concepts/overview.htm
You are an OCI specialist with expertise in API Gateway. Provide technical guidance on API creation, routes, integrations, and authentication.
Como criar um API Gateway no OCI?

Para criar um API Gateway no OCI:

**Pre-requisitos:**
- VCN com subnet
- Compartment com quota para API Gateway

**Via Console:**

1. Acesse Developer Services -> API Gateway
2. Selecione o compartment
3. Clique em "Create Gateway"
4. Configure:
   - Name: nome do gateway (ex: "api-gateway-prod")
   - Type: PUBLIC (acessivel via internet) ou PRIVATE (apenas VCN)
   - VCN: selecione sua VCN
   - Subnet: selecione subnet
   - [MUTABLE] Gateway publico tem custo por hora
5. Clique em "Create"

**Via CLI:**
oci api-gateway gateway create \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --display-name "api-gateway-prod" \
  --endpoint-type PUBLIC \
  --subnet-id <subnet-ocid>

**Criar uma API (deployment):**

1. No gateway criado, clique em "Create Deployment"
2. Configure:
   - Name: nome da API
   - Path prefix: /api/v1
3. Defina routes:
   - Path: /hello
   - Methods: GET
   - Type: HTTP Backend ou Oracle Functions
   - URL: backend endpoint
4. Clique em "Create"

**Testar a API:**
# Obter endpoint do gateway
oci api-gateway gateway get --gateway-id <gateway-ocid>

# Testar
curl https://<gateway-endpoint>/api/v1/hello

**[MUTABLE]** API Gateway: ~$0.05/hora + chamadas de API.

Referencia: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/APIGateway/Tasks/apigateway-getting-started.htm
You are an OCI specialist with expertise in API Gateway. Provide technical guidance on API creation, routes, integrations, and authentication.
Como configurar routes para OCI Functions no API Gateway?

Configurar routes para OCI Functions no API Gateway:

**1. Criar deployment com route para Function:**

Via Console:
1. API Gateway -> clique no gateway -> Deployments
2. Create Deployment
3. Configure:
   - Name: "api-functions"
   - Path prefix: /api
4. Adicionar route:
   - Path: /process
   - Methods: POST
   - Type: Oracle Functions
   - Application: selecione a application
   - Function: selecione a function
5. Clique em "Create"

**2. Via specification (OpenAPI):**

api-spec.yaml:
openapi: 3.0.1
info:
  title: API com Functions
  version: 1.0.0
paths:
  /api/process:
    post:
      x-oci-function:
        applicationId: "<app-ocid>"
        functionName: "process-data"
      responses:
        "200":
          description: Success
  /api/status:
    get:
      x-oci-function:
        applicationId: "<app-ocid>"
        functionName: "get-status"
      responses:
        "200":
          description: Success

**3. Deploy via CLI:**
oci api-gateway deployment create \
  --gateway-id <gateway-ocid> \
  --display-name "api-functions" \
  --path-prefix "/api" \
  --specification file://api-spec.yaml

**4. Passar request para function:**

A function recebe o request body automaticamente:
from fdk import response
import json

def handler(ctx, data):
    body = json.loads(data.getvalue().decode())
    return response.Response(
        ctx,
        response_data=json.dumps({"result": "processed"}),
        headers={"Content-Type": "application/json"}
    )

**5. Path parameters:**
paths:
  /api/users/{userId}:
    get:
      x-oci-function:
        applicationId: "<app-ocid>"
        functionName: "get-user"
      parameters:
        - name: userId
          in: path
          required: true

**6. Boas praticas:**
- Use path prefix para versionamento (/api/v1, /api/v2)
- Separe routes por recurso
- Configure CORS se necessario
- [CHECK DOCS] Verifique limites de routes por deployment

Referencia: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/APIGateway/Tasks/apigateway-using-deployments.htm
You are an OCI specialist with expertise in API Gateway. Provide technical guidance on API creation, routes, integrations, and authentication.
Como configurar autenticacao no API Gateway do OCI?

Autenticacao no API Gateway do OCI:

**1. API Keys:**

Via Console:
1. API Gateway -> Gateway -> Developer Portal
2. Create Application
3. Gere API key
4. Configure route para requerer API key

Via specification:
openapi: 3.0.1
paths:
  /api/protected:
    get:
      security:
        - api_key: []
      x-oci-function:
        applicationId: "<app-ocid>"
        functionName: "protected-handler"
securityDefinitions:
  api_key:
    type: apiKey
    name: Authorization
    in: header

**2. OAuth 2.0:**

securityDefinitions:
  oauth2:
    type: oauth2
    flow: implicit
    authorizationUrl: "https://identity.oraclecloud.com/"
    scopes:
      read: "Read access"
      write: "Write access"
paths:
  /api/protected:
    get:
      security:
        - oauth2: [read]

**3. JWT Validation:**

securityDefinitions:
  jwt:
    type: apiKey
    name: Authorization
    in: header
    x-oci-jwt:
      issuer: "https://identity.oraclecloud.com"
      audience: "<app-ocid>"
      publicKeys:
        - "https://identity.oraclecloud.com/.well-known/jwks.json"

**4. Mutual TLS (mTLS):**

mutualTls:
  mode: REQUIRED
  trust:
    certificateAuthorities:
      - "<ca-ocid>"

**5. IAM Authentication:**

Para APIs internas:
paths:
  /api/internal:
    get:
      x-oci-iam:
        required: true

**6. Configurar via CLI:**
oci api-gateway deployment create \
  --gateway-id <gateway-ocid> \
  --display-name "auth-api" \
  --path-prefix "/api" \
  --specification file://auth-spec.yaml

**7. Boas praticas:**
- Use HTTPS sempre
- Rotacao periodica de API keys
- Use OAuth2/JWT para aplicacoes externas
- mTLS para comunicacao service-to-service
- [CHECK DOCS] Verifique tipos de auth suportados

Referencia: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/APIGateway/Tasks/apigateway-using-authentication.htm
You are an OCI specialist with expertise in API Gateway. Provide technical guidance on API creation, routes, integrations, and authentication.
Como configurar rate limiting e throttling no API Gateway?

Rate limiting e throttling no API Gateway:

**1. Rate limiting por deployment:**

Via specification:
x-oci-rate-limiting:
  rateKey: CLIENT_IP
  rateInRequestsPerSecond: 100

**2. Rate limiting por route:**

paths:
  /api/search:
    get:
      x-oci-rate-limiting:
        rateKey: CLIENT_IP
        rateInRequestsPerSecond: 10
      x-oci-function:
        applicationId: "<app-ocid>"
        functionName: "search"

**3. Tipos de rate key:**

| Rate Key | Descricao |
|----------|----------|
| CLIENT_IP | Baseado no IP do cliente |
| API_KEY | Baseado na API key |
| AUTHORIZER | Baseado no resultado do authorizer |

**4. Rate limiting via Console:**

1. API Gateway -> Deployment -> Edit
2. Rate Limiting:
   - Enable rate limiting
   - Rate key: CLIENT_IP ou API_KEY
   - Rate: requests por segundo
3. Salve

**5. Response quando rate limited:**
{
  "code": "TooManyRequests",
  "message": "Rate limit exceeded"
}
HTTP Status: 429
Headers:
- Retry-After: segundos para tentar novamente
- X-RateLimit-Limit: limite configurado
- X-RateLimit-Remaining: requests restantes

**6. Boas praticas:**
- Configure limites diferentes por endpoint
- Endpoints pesados: limite menor
- Endpoints leves: limite maior
- Monitore rate limit hits
- [CHECK DOCS] Verifique limites maximos do API Gateway

Referencia: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/APIGateway/Tasks/apigateway-using-rate-limiting.htm
You are an OCI specialist with expertise in API Gateway. Provide technical guidance on API creation, routes, integrations, and authentication.
Como integrar API Gateway com backend HTTP externo?

Integracao do API Gateway com backend HTTP:

**1. Configurar route HTTP:**

Via specification:
openapi: 3.0.1
info:
  title: API com Backend HTTP
  version: 1.0.0
paths:
  /api/users:
    get:
      x-oci-backend:
        url: "https://backend.exemplo.com/api/users"
        type: HTTP_BACKEND
      responses:
        "200":
          description: Success
    post:
      x-oci-backend:
        url: "https://backend.exemplo.com/api/users"
        type: HTTP_BACKEND
      responses:
        "201":
          description: Created

**2. Via Console:**
1. API Gateway -> Deployment -> Create
2. Add route:
   - Path: /api/users
   - Methods: GET, POST
   - Type: HTTP Backend
   - URL: https://backend.exemplo.com/api/users
3. Configure:
   - Timeout: 30 segundos (padrao)
   - Retries: numero de tentativas
4. Clique em "Create"

**3. Path forwarding:**
paths:
  /api/{proxy+}:
    x-oci-backend:
      url: "https://backend.exemplo.com/{proxy}"
      type: HTTP_BACKEND

**4. Headers e query parameters:**

O API Gateway forward automaticamente:
- Headers do cliente para o backend
- Query parameters
- Request body

**5. Backend authentication:**

Se o backend requer auth:
x-oci-backend:
  url: "https://backend.exemplo.com/api"
  type: HTTP_BACKEND
  headers:
    Authorization: "Bearer <token>"
    X-API-Key: "<key>"

**6. Private backend:**

Para backends na VCN:
- Use API Gateway privado
- Backend na mesma VCN ou com conectividade
- Configure NSGs para permitir trafego

**7. Timeout e retries:**
x-oci-backend:
  url: "https://backend.exemplo.com/api"
  type: HTTP_BACKEND
  timeoutInSeconds: 60
  connectTimeoutInSeconds: 10
  retries: 3

Referencia: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/APIGateway/Tasks/apigateway-using-backend.htm
You are an OCI specialist with expertise in API Gateway. Provide technical guidance on API creation, routes, integrations, and authentication.
Como resolver erro 502 do API Gateway?

Troubleshooting de erro 502 (Bad Gateway) no API Gateway:

**O que significa:**
- 502 = API Gateway nao conseguiu comunicacao com o backend
- O gateway esta funcionando, mas o backend retornou resposta invalida

**1. Causas comuns:**

**a) Backend indisponivel:**
- Servico backend esta down
- URL incorreta
- DNS nao resolve

**b) Timeout:**
- Backend demora mais que o timeout configurado
- Default: 30 segundos

**c) SSL/TLS:**
- Certificado invalido no backend
- Protocolo TLS incompativel

**d) Response invalido:**
- Backend retorna response mal formatado
- Headers invalidos

**2. Diagnosticar:**

# Testar backend diretamente
curl -v https://backend.exemplo.com/api/health

# Verificar logs do API Gateway
oci logging search logs \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --search-query "search "oci_apigateway" | filter statusCode = 502"

# Verificar metricas
oci monitoring metric-data summarize \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --namespace "oci_apigateway" \
  --query-text "5xxErrorCount[5m].sum()"

**3. Solucoes:**

| Causa | Solucao |
|-------|--------|
| Backend down | Verificar health do backend |
| Timeout | Aumentar timeoutInSeconds |
| SSL error | Verificar certificado do backend |
| DNS | Verificar resolucao de nomes |
| Network | Verificar NSGs e security lists |

**4. Aumentar timeout:**
x-oci-backend:
  url: "https://backend.exemplo.com/api"
  timeoutInSeconds: 60

**5. Configurar health check:**
# Verificar se backend esta respondendo
curl -f https://backend.exemplo.com/health || echo "Backend down"

**6. Monitorar:**
- Configure alarmes para 5xx errors
- Monitore latency do backend
- [CHECK DOCS] Verifique limites de timeout

Referencia: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/APIGateway/Tasks/apigateway-troubleshooting.htm
You are an OCI specialist with expertise in API Gateway. Provide technical guidance on API creation, routes, integrations, and authentication.
Como configurar CORS no API Gateway do OCI?

Configuracao de CORS no API Gateway:

**1. Via specification (OpenAPI):**

openapi: 3.0.1
info:
  title: API com CORS
  version: 1.0.0
paths:
  /api/data:
    get:
      x-oci-cors:
        allowedOrigins:
          - "https://app.exemplo.com"
          - "https://admin.exemplo.com"
        allowedMethods:
          - GET
          - POST
          - PUT
          - DELETE
        allowedHeaders:
          - Content-Type
          - Authorization
        exposeHeaders:
          - X-Request-Id
        maxAgeInSeconds: 3600
        allowCredentials: true
      x-oci-function:
        applicationId: "<app-ocid>"
        functionName: "data-handler"

**2. Via Console:**

1. API Gateway -> Deployment -> Edit
2. CORS Policies:
   - Allowed Origins: dominios permitidos
   - Allowed Methods: GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, OPTIONS
   - Allowed Headers: headers permitidos
   - Expose Headers: headers expostos ao browser
   - Max Age: cache do preflight (segundos)
   - Allow Credentials: true/false
3. Salve

**3. CORS global (todas as routes):**

x-oci-cors:
  allowedOrigins:
    - "*"
  allowedMethods:
    - GET
    - POST
    - OPTIONS
  allowedHeaders:
    - Content-Type
    - Authorization
  maxAgeInSeconds: 86400

**4. Preflight (OPTIONS) handling:**

O API Gateway responde automaticamente a OPTIONS:
curl -X OPTIONS https://<gateway-endpoint>/api/data \
  -H "Origin: https://app.exemplo.com" \
  -H "Access-Control-Request-Method: POST" \
  -v

# Response esperado:
# Access-Control-Allow-Origin: https://app.exemplo.com
# Access-Control-Allow-Methods: GET, POST, PUT, DELETE
# Access-Control-Max-Age: 3600

**5. Boas praticas:**
- Nao use "*" para allowedOrigins em producao
- Especifique dominios exatos
- Configure allowCredentials=false se nao precisar de cookies
- [CHECK DOCS] Verifique suporte atual de CORS

Referencia: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/APIGateway/Tasks/apigateway-using-cors.htm
You are an OCI specialist with expertise in API Gateway. Provide technical guidance on API creation, routes, integrations, and authentication.
Como monitorar metricas e configurar alertas para o API Gateway?

Monitoramento do API Gateway:

**1. Metricas nativas (OCI Monitoring):**

Metricas disponiveis:
- RequestCount: numero de requests
- Latency: tempo de resposta (avg, p50, p95, p99)
- 4xxErrorCount: erros de cliente
- 5xxErrorCount: erros de servidor
- RequestSize: tamanho do request
- ResponseSize: tamanho da response

**2. Configurar alarmes:**

# Alarme para 5xx errors
oci monitoring alarm create \
  --display-name "API-5xx-Errors" \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --namespace "oci_apigateway" \
  --query "5xxErrorCount[5m].sum()" \
  --threshold 10 \
  --comparison-operator GREATER_THAN \
  --notification-topic-id <topic-ocid>

# Alarme para latency alta
oci monitoring alarm create \
  --display-name "API-High-Latency" \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --namespace "oci_apigateway" \
  --query "Latency[5m].mean()" \
  --threshold 5000 \
  --comparison-operator GREATER_THAN \
  --notification-topic-id <topic-ocid>

**3. Logging:**

- Logs automaticos de cada request
- Inclui: method, path, status code, latency
- Configure log groups no Console

oci logging search logs \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --search-query "search "oci_apigateway" | filter statusCode >= 400"

**4. Dashboards recomendados:**

**Performance:**
- Request count por minuto
- Latency p50, p95, p99
- Error rate (4xx, 5xx)
- Throughput (bytes/s)

**Business:**
- Requests por endpoint
- Top clients por API key
- Peak hours

**5. APM Integration:**
- Trace distribuido com OCI APM
- Correlacione API Gateway com backend
- Identifique bottlenecks end-to-end

**6. Alertas criticos:**
- 5xx error rate > 1%
- Latency p99 > 5 segundos
- Request count drop > 50% (possivel outage)
- 429 rate limit hits aumentando

Referencia: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/APIGateway/Tasks/apigateway-monitoring.htm
You are an OCI specialist with expertise in API Gateway. Provide technical guidance on API creation, routes, integrations, and authentication.
Como configurar request/response transformation no API Gateway?

Request/Response transformation no API Gateway:

**1. Request transformation:**

Via specification:
paths:
  /api/users:
    post:
      x-oci-request-transformation:
        headers:
          set:
            X-Forwarded-For: "${request.headers['X-Real-IP']}"
            X-Request-Id: "${request.headers['X-Request-Id']}"
          add:
            X-API-Version: "v1"
          remove:
            - X-Internal-Header
        queryParameters:
          set:
            format: "json"
        body:
          template: |
            {
              "data": ${request.body},
              "metadata": {
                "timestamp": "${request.headers['X-Timestamp']}"
              }
            }
      x-oci-function:
        applicationId: "<app-ocid>"
        functionName: "create-user"

**2. Response transformation:**

paths:
  /api/users:
    get:
      x-oci-response-transformation:
        headers:
          set:
            Cache-Control: "max-age=300"
            X-API-Version: "v1"
          add:
            X-Response-Time: "${response.headers['X-Processing-Time']}"
        body:
          template: |
            {
              "status": "success",
              "data": ${response.body},
              "meta": {
                "count": ${response.body.length}
              }
            }
      x-oci-backend:
        url: "https://backend.exemplo.com/users"

**3. Via Console:**

1. API Gateway -> Deployment -> Edit route
2. Request Transformation:
   - Headers: set, add, remove
   - Query parameters: set, add, remove
   - Body: template JSON
3. Response Transformation:
   - Headers: set, add, remove
   - Body: template JSON
4. Salve

**4. Use cases comuns:**

**Adicionar headers de autenticacao:**
x-oci-request-transformation:
  headers:
    set:
      Authorization: "Bearer ${request.headers['X-Internal-Token']}"

**Wrapper de response:**
x-oci-response-transformation:
  body:
    template: |
      {
        "success": true,
        "data": ${response.body}
      }

**5. Variaveis disponiveis:**

- ${request.headers['name']}
- ${request.body}
- ${request.queryParams['name']}
- ${response.headers['name']}
- ${response.body}
- ${response.statusCode}

**6. Boas praticas:**
- Documente transformations aplicadas
- Teste transformations antes de producao
- Use para padronizar APIs

You are an OCI specialist with expertise in API Gateway. Provide technical guidance on API creation, routes, integrations, and authentication.
Como configurar caching de responses no API Gateway?

Caching de responses no API Gateway:

**1. Response Cache:**

Via specification:
paths:
  /api/products:
    get:
      x-oci-response-cache:
        isEnabled: true
        ttlInSeconds: 300
        cacheKey:
          includeHeaders:
            - Accept
          includeQueryParams:
            - category
            - page
      x-oci-backend:
        url: "https://backend.exemplo.com/products"
        type: HTTP_BACKEND

**2. Configurar via Console:**

1. API Gateway -> Deployment -> Edit route
2. Response Cache:
   - Enable: Yes
   - TTL: 300 segundos (5 minutos)
   - Cache Key: headers e query params para incluir
3. Salve

**3. Cache invalidation:**

# Invalidar cache via API
curl -X POST https://<gateway-endpoint>/api/admin/cache/invalidate \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <admin-token>" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"path": "/api/products"}'

**4. Cache headers:**

O API Gateway respeita headers de cache do backend:
- Cache-Control: max-age=300
- ETag: para validacao
- Last-Modified: para validacao

**5. Conditional requests:**

O cache suporta conditional requests:
- If-None-Match: valida com ETag
- If-Modified-Since: valida com Last-Modified
- Response 304 Not Modified quando cache valido

**6. Boas praticas:**
- Cache apenas GET requests
- TTL curto para dados que mudam frequentemente
- TTL longo para dados estaticos
- Configure cache key apropriadamente
- Monitore hit rate do cache
- [CHECK DOCS] Verifique limites de cache storage

Referencia: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/APIGateway/Tasks/apigateway-using-caching.htm
You are an OCI specialist with expertise in OCI Functions. Provide technical guidance on function creation, deployment, and invocation.
Como criar e fazer deploy de uma function no OCI Functions?

Para criar e fazer deploy de uma function no OCI Functions:

**Pre-requisitos:**
- Docker instalado
- Fn Project CLI instalado
- OCI CLI configurado
- Application criada no OCI Functions

**1. Criar Application no OCI:**

Via Console:
1. Developer Services -> Functions -> Applications
2. Clique em "Create Application"
3. Configure:
   - Name: nome da aplicacao
   - VCN: selecione VCN
   - Subnets: selecione subnets
   - Logging: configure log group
4. Clique em "Create"

Via CLI:
oci fn application create \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --display-name "minha-app" \
  --subnet-ids '["<subnet-ocid>"]'

**2. Criar function localmente:**

# Inicializar projeto Python
mkdir minha-function && cd minha-function
fn init --runtime python minha-function

# Estrutura criada:
# func.py - codigo da function
# func.yaml - configuracao
# requirements.txt - dependencias

**3. Implementar a function:**

func.py:
import io
import json
from fdk import response

def handler(ctx, data: io.BytesIO = None):
    body = json.loads(data.getvalue().decode()) if data else {}
    name = body.get("name", "World")
    return response.Response(
        ctx,
        response_data=json.dumps({"message": f"Hello, {name}!"}),
        headers={"Content-Type": "application/json"}
    )

**4. Deploy:**

# Login no OCIR
fn registry login

# Deploy
fn deploy --app minha-app --no-bump

# Verificar
fn list functions minha-app

**5. Invocar:**
# Via Fn CLI
echo '{"name": "OCI"}' | fn invoke minha-app minha-function

# Via HTTP (invoke endpoint)
curl -X POST https://<invoke-endpoint>/t/<tenancy>/minha-app/minha-function \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"name": "OCI"}'

**[MUTABLE]** Precos: pay-per-invocation com free tier mensal.

Como configurar variaveis de ambiente e secrets em OCI Functions?

Configuracao de variaveis de ambiente e secrets em OCI Functions:

**1. Variaveis de ambiente via Console:**

1. Functions -> Applications -> clique na aplicacao
2. Configuration -> Add Configuration
3. Adicione key-value pairs:
   - DB_HOST: mysql.prod.svc
   - LOG_LEVEL: info
   - API_KEY: valor

**2. Variaveis via CLI:**
oci fn application update \
  --application-id <app-ocid> \
  --config '{"DB_HOST": "mysql.prod.svc", "LOG_LEVEL": "info"}'

**3. Usar na function (Python):**
import os
from fdk import response

def handler(ctx, data):
    db_host = os.environ.get('DB_HOST', 'localhost')
    log_level = os.environ.get('LOG_LEVEL', 'warning')
    
    return response.Response(
        ctx,
        response_data=f"DB: {db_host}, Log: {log_level}"
    )

**4. Secrets via OCI Vault:**

# Criar secret no Vault
oci vault secret create-base64 \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --vault-id <vault-ocid> \
  --secret-name "db-password" \
  --secret-content-content '<base64-password>'

# Referenciar na function
oci fn application update \
  --application-id <app-ocid> \
  --config '{
    "DB_PASSWORD": "ocid1.vaultsecret.oc1..<secret-ocid>"
  }'

**5. Resolver secret na function:**
import oci
import os

def get_secret_content(secret_ocid):
    config = oci.config.from_file()
    client = oci.secrets.SecretsClient(config)
    response = client.get_secret_bundle(secret_ocid)
    import base64
    return base64.b64decode(
        response.data.secret_bundle_content.content
    ).decode()

def handler(ctx, data):
    secret_id = os.environ.get('DB_PASSWORD')
    if secret_id and secret_id.startswith('ocid1'):
        password = get_secret_content(secret_id)
    else:
        password = secret_id
    return response.Response(ctx, response_data="OK")

**6. Boas praticas:**
- Nunca hardcode secrets no codigo
- Use OCI Vault para credenciais
- Rotacao periodica de secrets
- [CHECK DOCS] Verifique limites de configuracoes por aplicacao

Referencia: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Functions/Tasks/functionsconfiguringapps.htm

Como conectar uma OCI Function a um banco de dados Autonomous Database?

Conectar OCI Functions ao Autonomous Database:

**1. Pre-requisitos:**
- Autonomous Database criado
- Wallet baixado
- Function com runtime Python/Java/Node.js

**2. Configurar rede:**

A function precisa acessar o ADB:
- Se ADB e publico: function pode estar em subnet publica
- Se ADB e privado: function deve estar na mesma VCN ou ter conectividade

**3. Armazenar wallet como secret:**

# Compactar wallet
zip -r wallet.zip wallet/

# Codificar em base64
base64 -i wallet.zip -o wallet.b64

# Criar secret
oci vault secret create-base64 \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --vault-id <vault-ocid> \
  --secret-name "adb-wallet" \
  --secret-content-content '<base64-content>'

**4. Function Python com conexao ADB:**

func.py:
import os
import oracledb
import json
from fdk import response

def get_connection():
    wallet_path = "/tmp/wallet"
    oracledb.init_oracle_client(config_dir=wallet_path)
    
    return oracledb.connect(
        user="admin",
        password=os.environ.get('DB_PASSWORD'),
        dsn=os.environ.get('DB_DSN')
    )

def handler(ctx, data):
    try:
        conn = get_connection()
        cursor = conn.cursor()
        cursor.execute("SELECT * FROM produtos WHERE ROWNUM <= 10")
        rows = cursor.fetchall()
        
        result = [{"id": r[0], "nome": r[1]} for r in rows]
        
        return response.Response(
            ctx,
            response_data=json.dumps(result),
            headers={"Content-Type": "application/json"}
        )
    except Exception as e:
        return response.Response(
            ctx,
            response_data=json.dumps({"error": str(e)}),
            status_code=500
        )

**5. func.yaml:**
schema_version: 20180708
name: query-adb
version: 0.0.1
runtime: python
entrypoint: /python/bin/fdk /function/func.py handler
memory: 512
config:
  DB_DSN: "<adb-name>_medium"
  DB_PASSWORD: "ocid1.vaultsecret.oc1..<secret-ocid>"

**6. Consideracoes:**
- Timeout default: 30 segundos (configuravel ate 300s)
- Memory: minimo 128 MB, maximo 3072 MB
- [CHECK DOCS] Verifique versoes de driver Oracle suportadas

Referencia: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Functions/Tasks/functionsinvokingfunctions.htm
You are an OCI specialist with expertise in OCI Functions. Provide technical guidance on function creation, deployment, and invocation.
Como resolver timeout em OCI Functions?

Troubleshooting de timeout em OCI Functions:

**1. Entender limites:**
- Timeout default: 30 segundos
- Timeout maximo: 300 segundos (5 minutos)
- Se a function excede o timeout, retorna 504 Gateway Timeout

**2. Aumentar timeout:**

Via func.yaml:
timeout: 120

Via CLI:
oci fn function update \
  --function-id <function-ocid> \
  --timeout-in-seconds 120

**3. Otimizar performance:**

**a) Cold start:**
- Primeira invocacao e mais lenta (container initialization)
- Mitigue com:
  - Reduza tamanho da imagem Docker
  - Use multi-stage builds
  - Minimize dependencias

**b) Connection pooling:**
# Reuse conexoes entre invocacoes
_connection = None

def get_connection():
    global _connection
    if _connection is None:
        _connection = create_connection()
    return _connection

**c) Cache de dados:**
# Cache em memoria entre invocacoes
_cache = {}

def handler(ctx, data):
    key = data.get('key')
    if key not in _cache:
        _cache[key] = fetch_data(key)
    return _cache[key]

**4. Diagnosticar:**

# Verificar logs
oci fn function get --function-id <function-ocid>

# Verificar metricas
oci monitoring metric-data summarize \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --namespace "oci_functions" \
  --query-text "FunctionDuration[1m].mean()"

**5. Padroes para funcoes longas:**

**Async execution:**
def handler(ctx, data):
    # Enqueue para processamento assincrono
    enqueue_job(data)
    return {"status": "queued", "jobId": job_id}

**Chunking:**
- Divida processamento em multiplas functions
- Use OCI Streaming ou Queue como intermediario

**6. Monitorar:**
- Configure alarmes para duration > threshold
- Monitore cold start frequency
- [CHECK DOCS] Verifique limites atualizados de timeout

Referencia: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Functions/Tasks/functionsconfiguringfunctions.htm

Como fazer versionamento e gerenciamento de ciclo de vida de OCI Functions?

Versionamento de OCI Functions:

**1. Versionamento com Fn Project:**

# Incrementar versao
fn bump

# Deploy com versao especifica
fn deploy --app minha-app

# Listar versoes
fn list functions minha-app

**2. Versionamento via OCI:**

Cada deploy cria uma nova versao da function:
oci fn function list \
  --application-id <app-ocid>

# Ver detalhes de versao
oci fn function get --function-id <function-ocid>

**3. Aliases e traffic splitting:**

OCI Functions suporta aliases para gerenciar versoes:
# Criar alias
oci fn function create-alias \
  --function-id <function-ocid> \
  --alias-name "production" \
  --version "1.0.0"

# Invocar via alias
curl -X POST https://<invoke-endpoint>/t/<tenancy>/minha-app/minha-function:production

**4. Canary deployment:**

# Traffic splitting entre versoes
oci fn function update \
  --function-id <function-ocid> \
  --traffic-split '{
    "1.0.0": 90,
    "1.1.0": 10
  }'

**5. CI/CD pipeline:**

stages:
  - type: BUILD
    name: Build Function
    buildPipelineStage:
      - type: FUNCTION_BUILD
        image: iad.ocir.io/<namespace>/minha-function:${BUILD_RUN_ID}
  
  - type: DEPLOY
    name: Deploy Function

      - type: FUNCTION_DEPLOY

**6. Boas praticas:**

Como monitorar metricas e configurar alertas para OCI Functions?

Monitoramento de OCI Functions:

**1. Metricas nativas (OCI Monitoring):**

Metricas disponiveis:
- FunctionInvocations: numero de invocacoes
- FunctionDuration: tempo de execucao
- FunctionErrors: numero de erros
- FunctionThrottles: throttling events
- MemoryUtilization: uso de memoria

**2. Configurar alarmes:**

# Alarme para erros
oci monitoring alarm create \
  --display-name "Functions-Errors" \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --namespace "oci_functions" \
  --query "FunctionErrors[5m].sum()" \
  --threshold 5 \
  --comparison-operator GREATER_THAN \
  --notification-topic-id <topic-ocid>

# Alarme para duration alta
oci monitoring alarm create \
  --display-name "Functions-Slow" \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --namespace "oci_functions" \
  --query "FunctionDuration[5m].mean()" \
  --threshold 10000 \
  --comparison-operator GREATER_THAN \
  --notification-topic-id <topic-ocid>

**3. Logging:**

- Logs automaticos para cada invocacao
- Configure log groups no Console
- Inclui: duration, memory, status code

oci logging search logs \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --search-query "search "oci_functions" | sort by datetime desc"

**4. Dashboards recomendados:**

**Performance:**
- Invocations por minuto
- Duration media e p99
- Error rate
- Throttle rate

**Recursos:**
- Memory utilization
- Concurrent executions
- Cold start frequency

**5. APM Integration:**
- Instale APM agent na function
- Trace distribuido entre services
- Identifique bottlenecks

**6. Alertas criticos:**
- Error rate > 1%
- Duration p99 > timeout * 0.8
- Throttle rate > 0
- Memory > 90% do limite

Referencia: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Functions/Tasks/functionsmonitoring.htm
You are an OCI specialist with expertise in OCI Functions. Provide technical guidance on function creation, deployment, and invocation.
Como troubleshootar cold start em OCI Functions?

Troubleshooting de cold start em OCI Functions:

**O que e cold start:**
- Primeira invocacao apos periodo de inatividade
- OCI precisa provisionar container e carregar runtime
- Latencia adicional: 1-10 segundos dependendo do runtime

**1. Medir cold start:**

import time
from fdk import response

def handler(ctx, data):
    start = time.time()
    # ... logica da function
    duration = time.time() - start
    
    return response.Response(
        ctx,
        response_data=json.dumps({
            "duration": duration,
            "is_cold": is_cold_start()
        })
    )

**2. Fatores que afetam cold start:**

| Fator | Impacto |
|-------|--------|
| Runtime | Python < Node.js < Java < .NET |
| Package size | Mais dependencias = mais lento |
| Memory | Mais memoria = mais rapido |
| Image size | Imagem Docker grande = mais lento |

**3. Otimizacoes:**

**a) Reduzir tamanho da imagem:**
# Multi-stage build
FROM python:3.11-slim as builder
RUN pip install --prefix=/install -r requirements.txt

FROM python:3.11-slim
COPY --from=builder /install /usr/local
COPY func.py /function/

**b) Lazy loading:**
def handler(ctx, data):
    import heavy_library  # Import sob demanda
    result = heavy_library.process(data)
    return response.Response(ctx, response_data=result)

**c) Aumentar memory:**
# func.yaml
memory: 1024  # Mais memoria = CPU proporcional

**d) Keep-alive pattern:**
# Invocar periodicamente para manter container ativo
# cron: invoke function a cada 5 minutos

**4. Monitorar:**
oci monitoring metric-data summarize \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --namespace "oci_functions" \
  --query-text "FunctionDuration[1m].mean()"

**5. Quando cold start importa:**
- APIs sincronas com SLA rigoroso
- Webhooks com timeout curto
- Interacoes em tempo real

**Quando NAO importa:**
- Processamento assincrono
- Batch jobs
- Event-driven processing

Referencia: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Functions/Tasks/functionsbestpractices.htm
You are an OCI specialist with expertise in OCI Functions. Provide technical guidance on function creation, deployment, and invocation.
Quais sao os runtimes suportados pelo OCI Functions e como escolher o certo?

Runtimes suportados pelo OCI Functions:

**Runtimes disponiveis:**

| Runtime | Versoes | Use Case |
|---------|---------|----------|
| Python | 3.8, 3.9, 3.10, 3.11 | Data processing, ML, automacao |
| Node.js | 16, 18, 20 | APIs, webhooks, event processing |
| Java | 11, 17, 21 | Enterprise, Spring Boot |
| Go | 1.19, 1.20, 1.21 | Alta performance, CLI tools |
| .NET | 6, 7, 8 | Enterprise, ASP.NET |
| Ruby | 3.0, 3.1, 3.2 | Web apps, scripting |
| Custom | Dockerfile | Qualquer linguagem |

**Escolher o runtime:**

**Python:**
- Melhor para: data processing, ML, automacao
- Vantagens: ecossistema rico, facil de usar
- Cold start: rapido

**Node.js:**
- Melhor para: APIs, webhooks, I/O bound
- Vantagens: async nativo, npm ecosystem
- Cold start: rapido

**Java:**
- Melhor para: enterprise, microsservicos complexos
- Vantagens: tipagem, performance JIT
- Cold start: mais lento (use GraalVM para melhorar)

**Go:**
- Melhor para: alta performance, CLI
- Vantagens: binary unico, cold start rapido
- Cold start: muito rapido

**Custom runtime:**
FROM fnproject/fn-java-fdk:jdk17-latest
COPY target/my-function.jar /function/
CMD ["com.example.MyFunction::handleRequest"]

**Consideracoes:**
- Cold start: Go/Python < Node.js < Java/.NET
- Memory: Java/.NET precisam de mais memoria
- Package size: Go (binary) < Python < Java
- [CHECK DOCS] Verifique versoes mais recentes suportadas

Referencia: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Functions/Tasks/functionscreatingfunctions.htm

Como integrar OCI Functions com OCI Events para processamento assincrono?

Integracao OCI Functions com OCI Events:

**1. OCI Events Service:**

O Events Service detecta mudancas em recursos OCI e dispara functions:

**2. Criar regra de evento:**

Via Console:
1. Developer Services -> Events Service -> Rules
2. Create Rule
3. Configure:
   - Display Name: nome da regra
   - Description: descricao
   - Compartment: onde monitorar eventos
4. Event Types:
   - Exemplo: com.oraclecloud.objectstorage.createobject
   - Exemplo: com.oraclecloud.database.autonomousdatabase.lifecyclechanged
5. Actions:
   - Action Type: Functions
   - Application: selecione a application
   - Function: selecione a function
6. Clique em "Create Rule"

**3. Via CLI:**
oci events rule create \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --display-name "process-new-files" \
  --condition '{"eventType":["com.oraclecloud.objectstorage.createobject"]}' \
  --actions '{"actions":[{"actionType":"ONS","isEnabled":true}]}'

**4. Function handler para eventos:**
import json
from fdk import response

def handler(ctx, data):
    event = json.loads(data.getvalue().decode())
    
    # Extrair informacoes do evento
    event_type = event.get('eventType')
    resource_id = event.get('data', {}).get('resourceId')
    
    # Processar baseado no tipo de evento
    if 'objectstorage' in event_type:
        process_new_object(resource_id)
    elif 'database' in event_type:
        handle_db_change(resource_id)
    
    return response.Response(
        ctx,
        response_data=json.dumps({"status": "processed"}),
        headers={"Content-Type": "application/json"}
    )

**5. Tipos de eventos comuns:**

| Evento | Descricao |
|--------|----------|
| objectstorage.createobject | Novo arquivo no bucket |
| database.autonomousdatabase.lifecyclechanged | Mudanca de estado do ADB |
| compute.instance.lifecyclechanged | Mudanca de estado da instancia |
| loadbalancer.loadbalancer.lifecyclechanged | Mudanca no Load Balancer |

**6. Boas praticas:**
- Implemente idempotencia na function
- Configure retry para falhas
- Monitore eventos nao processados
- Use dead letter queue para eventos falhos
- [CHECK DOCS] Verifique lista completa de eventos suportados

You are an OCI specialist with expertise in OCI Functions. Provide technical guidance on function creation, deployment, and invocation.
Como configurar networking avancado para OCI Functions acessar recursos privados?

Networking avancado para OCI Functions:

**1. Functions em subnet privada:**

Ao criar a Application:
1. Selecione subnets privadas
2. Configure route table com NAT Gateway para saida
3. Configure Security Lists para acesso aos recursos

**2. Acessar recursos na VCN:**

# Configurar Application com subnets privadas
oci fn application create \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --display-name "minha-app" \
  --subnet-ids '["<private-subnet-ocid>"]'

**3. Acesso ao Autonomous Database privado:**

- Function na mesma VCN que o ADB
- Ou VCN peering entre VCNs
- Security List permitindo porta 1522 (TCPS)

**4. Acesso a OCI Services via Service Gateway:**

- Configure Service Gateway na VCN
- Route table apontando para Service Gateway
- Acesso a Object Storage, Vault, etc sem internet

**5. DNS Resolution:**

- Use DNS resolver da VCN
- Configure custom DNS se necessario
- Verifique resolv.conf no container

**6. NAT Gateway para saida:**

# Criar NAT Gateway
oci network nat-gateway create \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --vcn-id <vcn-ocid> \
  --display-name "functions-nat"

# Adicionar rota
oci network route-table route-rule add \
  --rt-id <rt-ocid> \
  --route-rules '[{"destination":"0.0.0.0/0","networkEntityId":"<nat-gw-ocid>"}]'

**7. Consideracoes de seguranca:**
- Nunca exponha functions publicamente sem necessidade
- Use NSGs para controle granular
- Configure WAF se necessario
- Monitore trafego de rede
- [CHECK DOCS] Verifique limites de networking para Functions

You are an OCI specialist with expertise in Block Volume. Provide technical guidance on block storage creation, attachment, performance tiers, and backups.
Como criar e attachar um Block Volume em uma instância OCI Linux?

Para criar e attachar um Block Volume a uma instância OCI:

**Passo 1: Criar o Block Volume**

**Via Console:**
1. Block Storage → Block Volumes → "Create Block Volume"
2. Selecione o compartment e o Availability Domain (deve ser o mesmo da instância)
3. Nomeie o volume (ex: "data-volume-01")
4. Escolha o tamanho (ex: 100 GB)
5. Selecione o performance tier (recomendado: Balanced)
6. Clique em "Create"

**Via CLI:**
```bash
oci bv volume create \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --availability-domain <AD> \
  --display-name "data-volume-01" \
  --size-in-gbs 100
```

**Passo 2: Attachar à instância**

**Via Console:**
1. Clique no volume criado → "Attach to Instance"
2. Selecione a instância
3. Escolha o tipo de attachment: "Paravirtualized" (recomendado) ou "iSCSI"
4. Clique em "Attach"

**Passo 3: Configurar no sistema operacional**
```bash
# Identifique o novo disco
lsblk
# Deve aparecer como /dev/sdb ou similar

# Para attachment paravirtualizado:
# O disco já aparece como /dev/sdb

# Para attachment iSCSI, execute os comandos fornecidos pelo console:
sudo iscsiadm -m node -o new -T <iqn> -p <ip>:3260
sudo iscsiadm -m node -o update -T <iqn> -n node.startup -v automatic
sudo iscsiadm -m node -T <iqn> -p <ip>:3260 -l

# Crie o filesystem
sudo mkfs.ext4 /dev/sdb

# Monte o volume
sudo mkdir -p /data
sudo mount /dev/sdb /data

# Adicione ao fstab para montar no boot
sudo blkid /dev/sdb
# Copie o UUID e adicione ao fstab:
echo 'UUID=<uuid> /data ext4 defaults,nofail 0 2' | sudo tee -a /etc/fstab
```

Doc: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Block/Tasks/managingvolumes.htm
You are an OCI specialist with expertise in Block Volume. Provide technical guidance on block storage creation, attachment, performance tiers, and backups.
Qual a diferença entre os performance tiers VP e BP do Block Volume no OCI?

O OCI Block Volume oferece dois modos de performance:

**Volume Performance (VP) - Antigo modelo:**
- Performance atrelada ao tamanho do volume
- 50 IOPS/GB para Balanced
- 75 IOPS/GB para Higher Performance
- Para obter mais IOPS, precisava aumentar o tamanho do volume
- Modelo legado, ainda suportado mas não recomendado para novos volumes

**Block Volume Performance (BP) - Novo modelo (recomendado):**
- Performance independente do tamanho do volume
- Escolha o tier de performance separadamente do tamanho
- Tiers disponíveis:
  - **Balanced**: 120K IOPS, 480 MB/s throughput por volume
  - **Higher Performance**: 250K IOPS, 680 MB/s throughput por volume
  - **Ultra High Performance**: 500K IOPS, 1000+ MB/s throughput por volume
- Permite ajustar performance sem redimensionar o volume
- [MUTABLE] Tiers mais altos têm custo adicional por GB

**Quando escolher cada tier:**
- **Balanced**: workloads gerais, dev/test, web servers
- **Higher Performance**: bancos de dados, aplicações enterprise
- **Ultra High Performance**: OLTP pesado, analytics, big data

**Alterando o tier de um volume existente:**
```bash
oci bv volume update \
  --volume-id <ocid> \
  --vpus-per-gb 20
# vpus-per-gb: 10 = Balanced, 20 = Higher, 30+ = Ultra High
```

**Comparação com outros clouds:**
- OCI BP ≈ AWS gp3 (performance independente de tamanho)
- OCI VP ≈ AWS gp2 (performance atrelada a tamanho)

You are an OCI specialist with expertise in Block Volume. Provide technical guidance on block storage creation, attachment, performance tiers, and backups.
Como configurar backup automático de Block Volumes no OCI?

Para configurar backups automáticos de Block Volumes:

**Via Console:**

1. **Criar uma Backup Policy:**
   - Block Storage → Block Volume Backup Policies → "Create Backup Policy"
   - Nome: "daily-backup-policy"
   - Adicione schedules:
     - **Daily**: retenção de 7 dias, hora: 02:00 UTC
     - **Weekly**: retenção de 4 semanas, dia: domingo
     - **Monthly**: retenção de 12 meses, dia: 1º do mês

2. **Aplicar a policy ao volume:**
   - Block Storage → Block Volumes → clique no volume
   - "Backup Policies" → "Assign Backup Policy"
   - Selecione a policy criada

**Via CLI:**
```bash
# 1. Crie a policy
oci bv backup-policy create \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --display-name "daily-backup-policy" \
  --schedules '[{
    "period": "ONE_DAY",
    "hourOfDay": 2,
    "dayOfMonth": "ALL",
    "dayOfWeek": "ALL",
    "month": "ALL",
    "offsetType": "NONE",
    "offsetNumber": 0,
    "backupType": "INCREMENTAL",
    "retentionSeconds": 604800
  }]'

# 2. Aplique ao volume
oci bv backup-policy-assignment create \
  --asset-id <volume-ocid> \
  --policy-id <policy-ocid>
```

**Backup manual:**
```bash
oci bv backup create \
  --volume-id <volume-ocid> \
  --display-name "manual-backup-$(date +%Y%m%d)" \
  --type FULL
```

**Verificando backups:**
```bash
oci bv backup list --compartment-id <ocid>
```

**Considerações:**
- Backups incrementais são mais eficientes após o primeiro full
- [MUTABLE] Backups são armazenados em Object Storage e cobrados separadamente
- Backups são regionais - para cross-region, use Volume Group Replication
- Políticas de backup não afetam performance do volume

You are an OCI specialist with expertise in Block Volume. Provide technical guidance on block storage creation, attachment, performance tiers, and backups.
Como clonar um Block Volume existente no OCI para criar um ambiente de teste?

Para clonar um Block Volume no OCI:

**Via Console:**
1. Block Storage → Block Volumes → clique no volume origem
2. "More Actions" → "Create Clone"
3. Defina:
   - Nome do clone (ex: "data-volume-test-clone")
   - Compartment de destino
   - Performance tier (pode ser diferente do original)
4. Clique em "Create Clone"

**Via CLI:**
```bash
oci bv volume create \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --availability-domain <AD> \
  --display-name "data-volume-test-clone" \
  --source-details '{
    "type": "volume",
    "id": "<source-volume-ocid>"
  }'
```

**Clonando a partir de um backup:**
```bash
oci bv volume create \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --availability-domain <AD> \
  --display-name "restored-from-backup" \
  --source-details '{
    "type": "backup",
    "id": "<backup-ocid>"
  }'
```

**Após o clone estar disponível:**
```bash
# Attachar à instância de teste
oci compute volume-attachment attach \
  --instance-id <test-instance-ocid> \
  --volume-id <cloned-volume-ocid> \
  --type paravirtualized

# Monte na instância de teste
ssh opc@<test-ip> "sudo mkdir -p /data-test && sudo mount /dev/sdb /data-test"
```

**Casos de uso:**
- Criar ambientes de teste com dados de produção
- Fork de databases para desenvolvimento
- Testar upgrades sem risco

**Considerações:**
- O clone é independente - mudanças não afetam o original
- [MUTABLE] Clone consome storage e é cobrado normalmente
- Clone dentro do mesmo AD é mais rápido
- Para cross-AD, o clone é feito via backup

You are an OCI specialist with expertise in Block Volume. Provide technical guidance on block storage creation, attachment, performance tiers, and backups.
Como resolver performance baixa em Block Volumes no OCI?

Diagnóstico e resolução de performance baixa em Block Volumes:

**1. Identifique o bottleneck:**
```bash
# Monitore IOPS e throughput
iostat -x 1 5

# Verifique queue depth
iostat -x | grep -E 'await|svctm|%util'

# Se %util está próximo de 100%, o volume está saturado
```

**2. Verifique o tier de performance:**
```bash
oci bv volume get --volume-id <ocid>
# Verifique 'vpus-per-gb':
# 10 = Balanced, 20 = Higher Performance, 30+ = Ultra High
```

**3. Soluções:**

**a) Aumente o tier de performance:**
```bash
oci bv volume update \
  --volume-id <ocid> \
  --vpus-per-gb 30
```
Sem downtime, mas leva alguns minutos para efetivar.

**b) Aumente o tamanho do volume (para volumes VP):**
```bash
oci bv volume update \
  --volume-id <ocid> \
  --size-in-gbs 500
```

**c) Use múltiplos volumes em RAID 0:**
```bash
# Crie 2+ volumes e combine
sudo mdadm --create /dev/md0 --level=0 --raid-devices=2 /dev/sdb /dev/sdc
sudo mkfs.ext4 /dev/md0
```
Isso dobra IOPS e throughput.

**d) Otimize o filesystem:**
```bash
# Use noatime para reduzir writes
sudo mount -o remount,noatime /data

# Ajuste I/O scheduler
echo 'none' | sudo tee /sys/block/sdb/queue/scheduler
```

**4. Monitore com OCI Metrics:**
- Volume Read/Write IOPS
- Volume Read/Write Throughput
- Volume Queue Depth

**Limites por volume [CHECK DOCS]:**
- Balanced: até 120K IOPS
- Higher Performance: até 250K IOPS
- Ultra High Performance: até 500K IOPS

You are an OCI specialist with expertise in Block Volume. Provide technical guidance on block storage creation, attachment, performance tiers, and backups.
Como configurar encryption com customer-managed keys para Block Volumes no OCI?

Para usar customer-managed keys (CMK) com Block Volumes:

**Pré-requisitos:**
- OCI Vault criado
- Master Encryption Key (MEK) criada no Vault

**Passo 1: Criar o Vault e Key (se não existir)**
```bash
# Criar vault
oci kms management vault create \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --display-name "storage-vault" \
  --vault-type DEFAULT

# Criar key
oci kms management key create \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --management-endpoint <vault-endpoint> \
  --display-name "block-volume-key" \
  --protection-mode SOFTWARE \
  --shape "AES-256"
```

**Passo 2: Criar Block Volume com CMK**

**Via Console:**
1. Block Storage → Block Volumes → "Create Block Volume"
2. Expanda "Encryption Options"
3. Selecione "Use customer-managed keys"
4. Escolha o Vault e a Key
5. Clique em "Create"

**Via CLI:**
```bash
oci bv volume create \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --availability-domain <AD> \
  --display-name "encrypted-volume" \
  --size-in-gbs 100 \
  --kms-key-id <key-ocid>
```

**Passo 3: Configurar políticas de acesso**
```yaml
# Policy para o serviço de Block Volume acessar o Vault
Allow service blockstorage to use vaults in compartment storage-compartment
Allow service blockstorage to use keys in compartment storage-compartment
```

**Convertendo volume existente para CMK:**
- Não é possível converter diretamente
- Crie um backup do volume
- Restaure o backup especificando a CMK

**Considerações de segurança:**
- CMK permite rotação de keys
- [CHECK DOCS] Desabilitar a key torna o volume inacessível
- Use Vault Service para audit de acesso às keys
- Considere HSM para workloads com compliance rigoroso

Doc: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Block/Concepts/overview.htm#Encryption
You are an OCI specialist with expertise in Block Volume. Provide technical guidance on block storage creation, attachment, performance tiers, and backups.
Como fazer resize de um Block Volume sem downtime no OCI?

O OCI permite expandir Block Volumes online, sem downtime:

**Passo 1: Expandir o volume no OCI**

**Via Console:**
1. Block Storage → Block Volumes → clique no volume
2. "More Actions" → "Edit"
3. Aumente o tamanho (ex: de 100 GB para 200 GB)
4. Clique em "Save Changes"

**Via CLI:**
```bash
oci bv volume update \
  --volume-id <ocid> \
  --size-in-gbs 200
```

**Passo 2: Expanda o filesystem no OS**

O OCI expande o block device, mas o filesystem precisa ser expandido manualmente:

**Para ext4:**
```bash
# Verifique o tamanho atual
df -h /data

# Expanda o filesystem (online, sem desmontar)
sudo resize2fs /dev/sdb

# Verifique o novo tamanho
df -h /data
```

**Para xfs:**
```bash
# XFS requer o mount point
sudo xfs_growfs /data
```

**Para btrfs:**
```bash
sudo btrfs filesystem resize max /data
```

**Passo 3: Automatize com cloud-init (opcional)**
```bash
# Adicione ao cloud-init para expandir automaticamente
runcmd:
  - resize2fs /dev/sdb
```

**Limites:**
- Tamanho mínimo: 50 GB
- Tamanho máximo: 32 TB
- [CHECK DOCS] Limites podem variar por região

**Importante:**
- Você pode aumentar, mas NÃO diminuir o tamanho de um volume
- A expansão é online - a instância não precisa ser parada
- O filesystem deve suportar online resize (ext4, xfs, btrfs suportam)

Doc: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Block/Tasks/resizingvolumes.htm
You are an OCI specialist with expertise in Block Volume. Provide technical guidance on block storage creation, attachment, performance tiers, and backups.
Como restaurar um Block Volume a partir de backup no OCI?

Para restaurar um Block Volume a partir de backup:

**Via Console:**

1. **Encontre o backup:**
   - Block Storage → Block Volume Backups
   - Filtre por compartment e data
   - Selecione o backup desejado

2. **Restaure como novo volume:**
   - Clique no backup → "Create Block Volume"
   - Defina o nome do volume restaurado
   - Selecione o Availability Domain
   - Escolha o performance tier
   - Clique em "Create"

**Via CLI:**
```bash
# 1. Liste backups disponíveis
oci bv backup list \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --lifecycle-state AVAILABLE

# 2. Restaure o backup como novo volume
oci bv volume create \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --availability-domain <AD> \
  --display-name "restored-volume" \
  --source-details '{
    "type": "backup",
    "id": "<backup-ocid>"
  }' \
  --vpus-per-gb 10
```

**Passo 3: Attachar e montar o volume restaurado**
```bash
# Attachar à instância
oci compute volume-attachment attach \
  --instance-id <instance-ocid> \
  --volume-id <restored-volume-ocid> \
  --type paravirtualized

# Montar na instância
ssh opc@<ip> "sudo mkdir -p /data-restored && sudo mount /dev/sdb /data-restored"
```

**Restaurando para substituir o volume original:**
1. Desmonte o volume original: `sudo umount /data`
2. Detach do volume original
3. Attach do volume restaurado
4. Monte no mesmo path

**Considerações:**
- O volume restaurado é uma cópia pontual do backup
- [MUTABLE] Restauração não gera custos extras além do storage do novo volume
- Backups incrementais são automaticamente consolidados na restauração
- Para disaster recovery, mantenha backups em outra região

Doc: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Block/Tasks/restoringavolume.htm
You are an OCI specialist with expertise in Block Volume. Provide technical guidance on block storage creation, attachment, performance tiers, and backups.
Como migrar um Block Volume entre Availability Domains no OCI?

Block Volumes são tied a um Availability Domain. Para migrar entre ADs:

**Método 1: Via Backup (recomendado)**

```bash
# 1. Crie um backup do volume no AD de origem
oci bv backup create \
  --volume-id <volume-ocid> \
  --display-name "pre-migration-backup"

# 2. Aguarde o backup completar
oci bv backup get --backup-id <backup-ocid>

# 3. Crie um novo volume no AD de destino a partir do backup
oci bv volume create \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --availability-domain <DESTINATION-AD> \
  --display-name "migrated-volume" \
  --source-details '{"type": "backup", "id": "<backup-ocid>"}'

# 4. Attachar o novo volume à instância no AD de destino
oci compute volume-attachment attach \
  --instance-id <new-instance-ocid> \
  --volume-id <new-volume-ocid> \
  --type paravirtualized
```

**Método 2: Via Volume Group (para múltiplos volumes)**

```bash
# 1. Crie um Volume Group
oci bv volume-group create \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --availability-domain <SOURCE-AD> \
  --display-name "migration-vg" \
  --volume-ids '["<vol-1-ocid>", "<vol-2-ocid>"]'

# 2. Clone o Volume Group para o AD de destino
oci bv volume-group create \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --availability-domain <DESTINATION-AD> \
  --display-name "migrated-vg" \
  --source-details '{"type": "volumeGroup", "id": "<vg-ocid>"}'
```

**Método 3: rsync (para dados, não block device)**
```bash
# Monte ambos os volumes e copie dados
rsync -avz --progress /data/ opc@<new-instance>:/data/
```

**Considerações:**
- Downtime é necessário durante a migração
- [MUTABLE] Backups cross-AD podem ter latência
- Verifique que a instância de destino está no mesmo AD do novo volume
- Volume Groups garantem consistência entre múltiplos volumes

Doc: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Block/Tasks/movingvolumes.htm
You are an OCI specialist with expertise in Block Volume. Provide technical guidance on block storage creation, attachment, performance tiers, and backups.
Como monitorar IOPS e throughput de Block Volumes no OCI?

Para monitorar IOPS e throughput de Block Volumes:

**Via OCI Monitoring Console:**
1. Monitoring → Metrics
2. Namespace: oci_blockstorage
3. Métricas disponíveis:
   - VolumeReadOps / VolumeWriteOps (IOPS)
   - VolumeReadBytes / VolumeWriteBytes (throughput)
   - VolumeQueueDepth
   - VolumeBurstBalance

**Via CLI:**
```bash
# IOPS de leitura
oci monitoring metric-data summarize-metrics-data \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --search-details '{
    "query": "VolumeReadOps[1m].count{volumeId=\"<volume-ocid>\"}"
  }'

# Throughput de escrita
oci monitoring metric-data summarize-metrics-data \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --search-details '{
    "query": "VolumeWriteBytes[1m].sum{volumeId=\"<volume-ocid>\"}"
  }'
```

**No lado do OS:**
```bash
# IOPS em tempo real
iostat -x 1 5

# Throughput
iostat -d -x 1 | grep -E 'Device|sdb'

# Verifique se está atingindo o limite
# Compare com os limites do tier:
# Balanced: 120K IOPS, 480 MB/s
# Higher Performance: 250K IOPS, 680 MB/s
# Ultra High Performance: 500K IOPS, 1000+ MB/s
```

**Configurando alertas:**
```bash
oci monitoring alarm create \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --display-name "high-iops" \
  --namespace oci_blockstorage \
  --query "VolumeReadOps[1m].count{volumeId=\"<volume-ocid>\"} > 100000" \
  --severity WARNING
```

**Considerações:**
- [MUTABLE] Limites de IOPS variam por tier e tamanho
- Burst balance indica se o volume está usando créditos de burst
- Queue depth alta indica bottleneck

You are an OCI specialist with expertise in File Storage. Provide technical guidance on NFS mounts, export options, and file system management.
Como criar um File Storage no OCI e montar em uma instância Linux?

Para criar e montar um File Storage (NFS) no OCI:

**Passo 1: Criar o File System**

**Via Console:**
1. File Storage → File Systems → "Create File System"
2. Selecione o compartment
3. Escolha o Availability Domain (deve ter instâncias no mesmo AD)
4. Nomeie o filesystem (ex: "shared-data")
5. Clique em "Create"

**Via CLI:**
```bash
oci fs file-system create \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --availability-domain <AD> \
  --display-name "shared-data"
```

**Passo 2: Criar o Mount Target**

**Via Console:**
1. File Storage → Mount Targets → "Create Mount Target"
2. Selecione a VCN e subnet
3. Defina o IP (ou deixe automático)
4. Associe ao File System criado
5. Clique em "Create"

**Via CLI:**
```bash
oci fs mount-target create \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --availability-domain <AD> \
  --display-name "shared-data-mt" \
  --subnet-id <subnet-ocid>
```

**Passo 3: Montar na instância Linux**
```bash
# Instale o cliente NFS
sudo dnf install -y nfs-utils  # Oracle Linux/RHEL
sudo apt install -y nfs-common  # Ubuntu

# Crie o diretório de mount
sudo mkdir -p /mnt/shared

# Monte o filesystem
sudo mount -t nfs <mount-target-ip>:/<export-path> /mnt/shared

# O export path é mostrado no console (ex: /shared-data)

# Adicione ao fstab para persistência
echo '<mount-target-ip>:/<export-path> /mnt/shared nfs defaults,_netdev 0 0' | sudo tee -a /etc/fstab

# Verifique
df -h /mnt/shared
```

**Requisitos de rede:**
- Security List deve permitir TCP/UDP 111, 2048-2050
- Instância e Mount Target devem estar na mesma VCN

Doc: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/File/Tasks/creatingfilesystems.htm
You are an OCI specialist with expertise in File Storage. Provide technical guidance on NFS mounts, export options, and file system management.
Como configurar export options para controle de acesso no OCI File Storage?

Export options controlam quem pode acessar o File Storage e com quais permissões:

**Via Console:**
1. File Storage → Mount Targets → clique no mount target
2. "Export Sets" → clique no export set
3. "Edit Export Options"
4. Adicione regras de export:

**Exemplo de configuração:**
```
Export 1:
  Path: /shared-data
  Source: 10.0.1.0/24
  Access: Read/Write
  Identity Squash: root
  Privileged Port: No

Export 2:
  Path: /shared-data/readonly
  Source: 10.0.2.0/24
  Access: Read Only
  Identity Squash: all
  Privileged Port: No
```

**Via CLI:**
```bash
oci fs export create \
  --export-set-id <export-set-ocid> \
  --path "/shared-data" \
  --export-options '[{
    "source": "10.0.1.0/24",
    "access": "READ_WRITE",
    "identitySquash": "ROOT",
    "requirePrivilegedSourcePort": false
  }, {
    "source": "10.0.2.0/24",
    "access": "READ_ONLY",
    "identitySquash": "ALL",
    "requirePrivilegedSourcePort": false
  }]'
```

**Opções de Identity Squash:**
- **None**: mantém o UID/GID original do cliente
- **Root**: mapeia root do cliente para nobody (segurança)
- **All**: mapeia todos os UIDs para nobody

**Boas práticas:**
- Use CIDR específico, não 0.0.0.0/0
- Root squash é recomendado para segurança
- Separe exports por nível de acesso
- [CHECK DOCS] Máximo de 1000 exports por mount target

Doc: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/File/Tasks/manageexports.htm
You are an OCI specialist with expertise in File Storage. Provide technical guidance on NFS mounts, export options, and file system management.
Como fazer backup de File Storage no OCI?

O OCI File Storage suporta backups via snapshots:

**Criando um snapshot manual:**

**Via Console:**
1. File Storage → Snapshots → "Create Snapshot"
2. Selecione o File System
3. Nomeie o snapshot (ex: "snapshot-2024-01-15")
4. Clique em "Create"

**Via CLI:**
```bash
oci fs snapshot create \
  --file-system-id <filesystem-ocid> \
  --name "snapshot-2024-01-15"
```

**Listando snapshots:**
```bash
oci fs snapshot list --file-system-id <filesystem-ocid>
```

**Restaurando a partir de um snapshot:**

O OCI File Storage não permite restore direto. Em vez disso:

```bash
# 1. Crie um novo file system
oci fs file-system create \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --availability-domain <AD> \
  --display-name "restored-fs"

# 2. Crie um mount target para o novo filesystem
oci fs mount-target create \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --availability-domain <AD> \
  --subnet-id <subnet-ocid> \
  --display-name "restored-mt"

# 3. Clone o snapshot para o novo filesystem
oci fs clone create \
  --file-system-id <new-filesystem-ocid> \
  --source-details '{"type": "snapshot", "id": "<snapshot-ocid>"}'

# 4. Monte o novo filesystem
sudo mount -t nfs <new-mt-ip>:/<export-path> /mnt/restored
```

**Automatizando snapshots:**
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# Cron job para snapshot diário
DATE=$(date +%Y%m%d)
oci fs snapshot create \
  --file-system-id <filesystem-ocid> \
  --name "auto-snapshot-$DATE"

# Cleanup de snapshots antigos (>30 dias)
oci fs snapshot list --file-system-id <filesystem-ocid> \
  --query "data[?\"time-created\"<\"$(date -v-30d +%Y-%m-%d)\"].id" \
  --output text | while read snap_id; do
  oci fs snapshot delete --snapshot-id $snap_id --force
done
```

**Considerações:**
- Snapshots são point-in-time consistent
- [MUTABLE] Snapshots são cobrados por GB armazenado
- Não afetam performance do filesystem ativo

Doc: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/File/Tasks/creatingsnapshots.htm
You are an OCI specialist with expertise in File Storage. Provide technical guidance on NFS mounts, export options, and file system management.
Como resolver problemas de mount NFS em instâncias OCI?

Troubleshooting de mount NFS no OCI File Storage:

**Problema 1: Connection refused**
```bash
# Verifique conectividade de rede
telnet <mount-target-ip> 2049
nc -zv <mount-target-ip> 2049

# Verifique Security Lists
# TCP/UDP 111 (portmapper), 2048-2050 (NFS) devem estar liberados
```

**Problema 2: Permission denied**
```bash
# Verifique export options
oci fs export list --export-set-id <export-set-ocid>

# Verifique se o IP da instância está no source CIDR
ip addr show

# Verifique identity squash
# Se identitySquash=ALL, todos os users viram nobody
ls -la /mnt/shared/
```

**Problema 3: Mount hangs**
```bash
# Use opções de mount mais robustas
sudo mount -t nfs -o hard,timeo=600,retrans=2,_netdev \
  <mount-target-ip>:/<export-path> /mnt/shared

# Verifique DNS resolution
nslookup <mount-target-ip>

# Verifique route table da subnet
oci network route-table list --vcn-id <vcn-ocid>
```

**Problema 4: Stale file handle**
```bash
# Force unmount
sudo umount -f /mnt/shared
sudo umount -l /mnt/shared  # lazy unmount

# Remonte
sudo mount -a
```

**Problema 5: Performance lenta**
```bash
# Use opções otimizadas
sudo mount -t nfs -o hard,intr,rsize=1048576,wsize=1048576,_netdev \
  <mount-target-ip>:/<export-path> /mnt/shared

# Verifique I/O stats
iostat -x 1 5
nfsiostat
```

**Diagnóstico geral:**
```bash
# Verifique mounts ativos
mount | grep nfs

# Verifique NFS stats
nfsstat -c

# Verifique logs
dmesg | grep nfs
tail -f /var/log/messages | grep nfs
```

Doc: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/File/Tasks/troubleshooting.htm

Como configurar acesso de múltiplas instâncias ao mesmo File Storage no OCI?

File Storage do OCI é projetado para acesso compartilhado por múltiplas instâncias:

**Configuração básica:**

1. **Crie o File System e Mount Target** (uma única vez):
```bash
oci fs file-system create \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --availability-domain <AD> \
  --display-name "shared-fs"

oci fs mount-target create \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --availability-domain <AD> \
  --subnet-id <subnet-ocid> \
  --display-name "shared-fs-mt"
```

2. **Monte em cada instância:**
```bash
# Em cada instância (web-1, web-2, web-3, etc.)
sudo mkdir -p /mnt/shared
sudo mount -t nfs <mount-target-ip>:/shared-fs /mnt/shared
```

**Configurando export para múltiplas subnets:**
```bash
oci fs export create \
  --export-set-id <export-set-ocid> \
  --path "/shared-fs" \
  --export-options '[{
    "source": "10.0.1.0/24",
    "access": "READ_WRITE",
    "identitySquash": "ROOT"
  }, {
    "source": "10.0.2.0/24",
    "access": "READ_WRITE",
    "identitySquash": "ROOT"
  }, {
    "source": "10.0.3.0/24",
    "access": "READ_ONLY",
    "identitySquash": "ALL"
  }]'
```

**Para OKE (Kubernetes):**
```yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: PersistentVolume
metadata:
  name: fss-pv
spec:
  capacity:
    storage: 1Ti
  accessModes:
    - ReadWriteMany
  nfs:
    server: <mount-target-ip>
    path: /shared-fs
```

**Considerações:**
- File Storage suporta RWX (ReadWriteMany) - múltiplos writers
- [CHECK DOCS] Performance escala com número de clientes
- Use lock mechanisms (flock) para coordenação de writes
- Ideal para shared home directories, content management, CI/CD artifacts

Doc: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/File/Tasks/creatingfilesystems.htm
